DISCOVERY 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS IN DEPARTMENT 302 FILED BEFORE JUNE 1, 2020 ARE OFF CALENDAR PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S GENERAL ORDERS. TO RE-SCHEDULE THE HEARING, PLEASE REFER TO THE PUBLIC NOTICE TO RESTORE SERVICES ISSUED ON MAY 27, 2020 AVAILABLE ON THE COURT'S WEBSITE AT HTTPS://SFSUPERIORCOURT.ORG/. (302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BONNIE WOLF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS ATTENDANCE AND TESTIMONY AT DEPOSITION; REQUEST FOR EVIDENCE, ISSUE AND MONETARY SANCTIONS - TO WHICH NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED - IS GRANTED. I ADOPT THE JUDGE PRO TEM'S REPORT AND RECOMMENDATIONS. PLAINTIFF DOROTHY LEONG SHALL TESTIFY AT A DEPOSITION AT A DATE AND TIME SPECIFIED BY DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL. PLAINTIFF IS PROHIBITED FROM ENTERING INTO EVIDENCE ANY MEDICAL-TREATMENT OR BILLING RECORDS. TESTIMONY AS TO PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES SHALL BE LIMITED TO HER DESCRIPTION OF THE INJURIES IDENTIFIED IN HER UNVERIFIED RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND SHALL NOT REFER TO ANY MEDICAL TREATMENT OR BILL. SANCTIONS OF $1,973.40 ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HER ATTORNEYS. (CCP 2023.010, 2023.030, 2025.430, 2025.450.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Attendance And Testimony At Deposition; Request For Evidence, Issue And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Caitlin J. Scott (424-231-2864; cscott@lpfletcherlaw.com) ofLAW OFFICES OF LOYST P. FLETCHER for Plaintiff.
Michael Daley (mike.daley.mugb@statefarm.com) for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BONNIE WOLF's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Attendance And Testimony At Deposition; Request For Evidence, Issue And Monetary Sanctions - to which no opposition was filed - is granted. I adopt the judge pro tem's report and recommendations. Plaintiff Dorothy Leong shall testify at a deposition at a date and time specified by defendant's counsel. Plaintiff is prohibited from entering into evidence any medical-treatment or billing records. Testimony as to plaintiff's injuries shall be limited to her description of the injuries identified in her unverified responses to form interrogatories and shall not refer to any medical treatment or bill. Sanctions of $1,973.40 are awarded against plaintiff and her attorneys. (CCP 2023.010, 2023.030, 2025.430, 2025.450.)

order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
